dependency: A shared geographic/organization identifier relationship: the identifier in one column corresponds to the named facility/organization in another (the same record uses the ID for the same hospital).
The identifier for an organization/facility determines the corresponding facility name and the facility address/street line; all three fields refer to the same facility record and must align.
The identifier for an organization/facility determines the corresponding facility name and the facility address/street line; all three fields refer to the same facility record and must align.
Facility-to-city dependency: the hospital name determines the city where it is located.

The street-address column appears paired with an additional address/neighborhood field; the placeholder/empty value indicates that when the primary street address is present, the auxiliary address component may be omitted or standardized.
City/state relationship: the city value in one column is the municipality within the state/province code/value in another (the postal location fields are consistent pairs).
A locality/state set determines the postal code; the city (locality), state, and ZIP/area code are linked and must correspond to the same geographic location.
State-to-zip relationship: the state value constrains the postal/ZIP code values in another column (ZIP is issued within the state shown).
ZIP-to-county/region relationship: the postal code value implies the associated county/region value (the county/region is consistent with the ZIP).

The healthcare setting/type column (e.g., acute care hospitals) is related to the ownership/payment-type classification; the facility type category constrains the plausible values of the organization-type category.

The organization/payment-type column (e.g., voluntary non-profit - private) semantically determines the categorical indicator column (e.g., yes/no eligibility/status aligned with that organizational type).

A higher-level domain/category label for a measurement area determines the set of valid measure codes; the specific measure identifier is a subtype within that domain (e.g., surgical infection prevention measures).
Condition-to-measure description dependency: the condition category in one column constrains the clinical process description in another to match that condition’s measure set.

Within a given measurement area and measure description, the numeric patient count depends on which measure is being evaluated; the described patient population determines the reported number of patients for that measure.

The structured code value specifies the exact clinical measure description in the accompanying text column (the code pn-3b corresponds to the blood-culture timing measure; pn-4 corresponds to smoking-cessation counseling).

Derived/composite measure identifier dependency: the abbreviated measure code in one column and the composite code in another use the same measure suffix (the composite appears to embed the state and measure code).

Within a given measurement area and measure description, the numeric patient count depends on which measure is being evaluated; the described patient population determines the reported number of patients for that measure.
The number of patients used as the denominator (or base population) determines the percent/result field’s value (a percentage summary derived from the count data for the same measure).
